items.question: Which dataset format defines supervised learning?
items.explanation: Supervised learning requires each training input x to have a known target y.

items.question: During inference on new data, what does the model need?
options: Features x and true label y
items.explanation: At prediction time, labels are unknown; the model outputs ŷ from x alone.

items.question: Predicting house sale price from past listings is an example of:
items.explanation: Each listing has a known numeric price label y paired with features x.

items.question: What provides the supervision signal during training?
options: Known labels y compared to predictions ŷ
items.answer: Known labels y compared to predictions ŷ
items.explanation: Loss L(ŷ, y) uses labels to guide parameter updates.

items.question: Spam detection trained on emails labeled spam/ham is:
items.explanation: Binary class labels on each email define the supervised training objective.

items.question: Why hold out a test set with labels in supervised learning?
options: To estimate generalization on unseen (x, y)
Because test sets must be unlabeled
To remove the need for a loss function
items.answer: To estimate generalization on unseen (x, y)
items.explanation: Metrics on held-out labeled data measure performance beyond memorizing training labels.
